candidates.stmt: My name is Jon. I have lived in the Brinscall and Withnell area for more than 20 years. I have a great appreciation for this area and its community. I enjoy fell running on the moors, playing my trumpet in a music group and canoeing on the canal. I have only recently become involved with the Green Party as I am passionate about environmental justice. I believe that the government’s action, both locally and nationally, on green issues is fundamental in protecting future generations. I am looking forward to giving people in the area the opportunity to vote Green.”

I am Chorley born and bred and ran the Broadband 4 Brinscall campaign back in 2002, helping to bring broadband to Brinscall and the surrounding villages for the first time. I have been monitoring air pollution in the Chorley borough since October 2017, lobbying both Chorley and Lancashire County Council to start monitoring particulate air pollution, and campaigning for Chorley Council to invest in real time monitors. I am passionate about low carbon solutions to help address the climate crisis such as electric cars, heat pumps and renewables.

I am active campaigner in Chorley South. I am always out and about,
speaking to residents and working hard on the things they tell me they
care most about. I have had significant success in ensuring improvements
to our streets and pavements.

I live in Chorley with my husband. I have been teaching languages and
employability skills to young people across Lancashire for almost 30
years, and leading on big-scale higher education projects. I speak
fluent Spanish and French, and in my free time enjoy gardening, walking
in our beautiful local countryside as well as working with the NHS and other
organisations on techniques for good mental health.

For the past five years, I have been an active member of the Chorley Green Party team, working hard to get the best for our amazing town.

Born and raised in Lancashire, I have grown up with the Chorley landmarks on my doorstep. To be able to enjoy Cuerden or Yarrow Valley on the doorstep, across to the gardens at Rivington or a wander through Astley Park is something we are so fortunate to have access to. Since having my two children though, I’ve become increasingly worried about the future we are leaving behind for these generations and the rise of both climate skeptics and populism in our collective decision-making.

I believe that local people should be making decisions for local issues, and sometimes it can feel hard to know how best to come about changing the status quo. I like to focus much of my spare time in grassroot projects where I believe my voice, with others, can be a real force for good.

Your Labour candidate for Clayton West & Cuerden Neville moved to Cuerden with his family twenty years ago, having served in the Royal Air Force for thirty years. Since then, Neville has worked in health, safety and environmental management and served as a councillor on Chorley Council from 2018 to 2021. Neville actively supported residents in Clayton West & Cuerden in getting a much needed, new doctors’ surgery on Preston Road. He has helped bring to account landfill site owners over release of obnoxious smells, and was instrumental in opposing the destruction of the beautiful Nell Lane by campaigning to stop selfish developers. Neville strives to help make Clayton West & Cuerden a better place in which to live by helping out with weekly litter picks, whilst endeavouring to safeguard our important green areas. Vote for Neville Whitham on Thursday 5th May Promoted by David Snape on behalf of Chorley Labour Paty, 92 Pall Mall, Chorley, PR7 2LB

As a teacher, I take a keen interest in education policy and provision for young people and families in our town. I am also passionate about improving our local NHS offer, especially mental health services. I believe that wellbeing should be at the heart of all that we do in this beautiful county, and we all have the right to access well-maintained green spaces as well as healthy food and clean air and water, free from pollution. I also take a keen interest in housing policy, having learned more this year about the strength of feeling locally about a number of proposed developments on greenbelt land in the Coppull area.

Chorley Borough Council is dominated by the two main parties. If elected as your Green councillor, I will be an advocate for you, and use my experience as an activist to push for a fairer and greener Chorley. As a grass roots party, we believe in listening to the wishes and needs of our residents, and unlike the other big parties, our candidates are free to vote with their conscience and to fully represent constituents’ views when making decisions which affect them. Along with our rapidly growing dedicated team of local volunteers, I will continue to work hard for our community all year round, not just at election time.

So I hope you will consider voting for me on the 7th May – every Green Party voice on our local Council means we can make a real difference to the old ‘business as usual’ in Chorley.

I am very proud of what we say in our Lib Dem constitution: “The Liberal Democrats exist to build and safeguard a free, fair and open society, in which we seek to balance the fundamental values of liberty, equality and community, and in which no-one shall be enslaved by poverty, ignorance or conformity”.

At the age of 25 I was elected as the youngest councillor on Preston Borough Council serving for 8 years. I have represented the party in three general elections, and have been part of the Southport Lib Dem team since 2010.

Southport faces challenges and, like so many seaside towns throughout the country, needs more support from the government, and from the local council. My priorities would be to do everything I can to boost jobs and the local economy, to support our NHS and schools, to protect our environment, and to help those in Southport who face poverty, disadvantage, or other hurdles. Everyone should have a fair chance to get on in life.

I also think MPs should be more open, transparent, honest and accountable. They should be more accessible to the people who elect them. And they should put the people and the country’s interests before their own and their party’s. We deserve a much better calibre of MPs than many of those we’ve got at the moment; we need people with courage and integrity.

My name’s Jonathan Close and I’m standing as your local Reform UK candidate for Lancashire County Council on Thursday 1st May.  To introduce myself; I’m 41 years old, was born and raised in Chorley, and I’ve lived here nearly my whole life.  I have a young family and currently run a software and IT company that’s based in Bolton.

Like so many, I am completely fed up with the state of politics in this country.  Our two-party system is broken and no longer caters to ordinary hardworking people.  A fresh approach is urgently needed.  We cannot continue to elect hundreds of career politicians to Westminster that do not understand or care about the issues we face.

At a local level I believe Lancashire County Council urgently needs reform.  Freedom of Information requests by Reform UK have revealed huge levels of debt, eye-watering interest payments, wasteful spending on pointless projects, huge unjustified salaries for Council bosses, DEI officers, rip-off and badly negotiated long-term contracts (including for pothole repair) and spending on green initiatives with dubious scientific value.  Reform UK will cut this waste to improve services and freeze your council tax bills.  Please see my web page for more information on local policies.

Overall, Reform UK have 84 honest, decent, caring and hardworking people like me standing in all districts of Lancashire.  We are highly motivated, serious, and aim to prove to the people of the north-west that we can ultimately change Lancashire and this country for the better.

Please do not underestimate how crucial the elections on 1st May are in the journey for national change.  We need local councillors in office to give us a strong platform to challenge the next General Election.  Whether you usually vote at local elections or not, I ask that you please do so on 1st May and support us.  I believe the future of our country now depends on it.

Thank you for taking the time to read this letter.  If you’d like to learn more about m

Now that the Statement of Persons Nominated has been published, I am pleased to announce that I am the official Labour Party candidate, standing in the Lancashire County Council elections for the division of Euxton, Buckshaw and Astley on Thursday 1st May 2025.

Now, here's a little bit about me...

I currently live in Astley Village, and have spent much of my life here, and I'm a former pupil of Buckshaw Primary School - more years ago than I care to remember! I also spent eight fab years living in Buckshaw Village until 2019.

I am the current Chair of Astley Village Parish Council, having been elected in May 2023 and re-elected in May 2024. I enjoy getting involved in various events to help improve our local community, including litter picks.

I served as a Borough Councillor in Euxton South between 2018 and 2021 and I also served on Euxton Parish Council between 2018 and 2022. In 2021, I proposed that Chorley Council create a Champion for Mental Health Awareness role and this was voted through unanimously. I am passionate about promoting positive mental health and wellbeing having struggled myself several years ago.
I work in a further education college in East Lancashire, and I am also a union representative, as well as a Staff Governor.

I will:

✅ Advocate for improved SEND (Special Educational Needs and Disabilities) provision in our schools - something which is close to my heart.
✅ Push for better quality and more efficient pothole repairs on our roads and pavements.
✅ Work collaboratively with Borough Councillors and local Police to report issues including anti-social behaviour, speeding and dangerous parking.

I have really enjoyed meeting and listening to residents in Euxton, Buckshaw Village and Astley Village over the past few months. Granted, we have not made it to every street just yet, but I'm hoping to meet many more people over the next few weeks. It is also important to say that we all have different views and opinions - I respect people's choice
